subject: Chemistry
title: Soap: Fat Meets Lye
description: A fast breakdown of Soap: Fat Meets Lye for builders.

hook.text: Soap is made by boiling animal fat with ash water. Humans have done this for over 4,000 years.

buildup.text: The reaction is called saponification. A strong base (lye) breaks fat into glycerol and fatty acid salts.

discovery.text: Soap molecules have a water-loving head and a grease-loving tail. They surround dirt and pull it into water.

twist.text: Until the 1800s, people rarely used soap for bathing. It was mainly for cleaning textiles.

climax.text: Handwashing with soap prevents millions of deaths from infection every year. It's the simplest lifesaver.

punchline.text: Fat plus lye equals soap. 4,000 years old and still saving lives.

quiz.question: What is saponification?
options: A reaction between fat and lye that produces soap
